calendar.matters:
ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-19-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Aug-16-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Motion to Apportion and Distribute Funds generated from the personal injury/survival lawsuit and claims against bankrupt asbestos defendant trusts.
Dept. 514. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Jennifer Alesio, Esq. of Brayton Purcell appeared to present the issues.
John S. Rueppel, Esq. appeared for Vanessa Klein, Successor-In-Interest to Charles Patterson, Deceased. Ms. Klein was not present for this morning's proceedings.
No appearance by Wrongful Death Heirs Donna Patterson or Jon Patterson.

The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, RULES that the following are entitled to distribution of all net funds from the survival/wrongful death lawsuit and claims against asbestos bankrupt defendant trusts negotiated after the January 29, 2015 death of Charles Patterson in the following proportions as follows:

Estate of Charles Patterson - 100%.
Donna Patterson - 0%.
Jon Patterson - 0%.

All future net funds derived from settlements entered into after the January 29, 2015 death of Charles Patterson shall be distributed in the percentages set forth on page 2, paragraph 1 of the Order approving the motion for apportionment.

Proposed Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds was submitted to and signed by the Court.

ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-20-2014 Set For Trial On Dec-12-14 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Aug-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Charles Patterson & Amy Patterson
(Refer to Sign-in Sheet for all other appearances.)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

No argument made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). No opposition filed. Trial is December 12, 2014 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is December 10, 2014. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice dictated by the C.C.P., unless plaintiffs stipulate otherwise. Time to respond to written discovery is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendant no later than September 26, 2014. Response to the demand is due no later than October 3, 2014. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating the best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by December 8, 2014 at 3:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on December 10, 2014. Fact discovery cut-off date is December 1, 2014. Expert discovery cut-off date is December 11, 2014. No later than September 3, 2014 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than September 3, 2014 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on October 22, 2014 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on December 12, 2014 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by December 10, 2014 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri l. Jackson, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
